title: Report export drops a required section when one field is empty
bug_description: A generated report omits a required summary section when one optional source field is blank, producing an incomplete deliverable.
expected_behavior: The required section should still appear with fallback text or an empty state that preserves document structure.
actual_behavior: The section disappears entirely, causing missing content and a broken report layout.

preconditions: A fixture report input exists where one optional upstream field is blank
The export command can run locally
steps: Generate the report using the fixture where the optional field is empty
Open or inspect the output and check whether the required summary section exists
reproduction.expected_behavior: The report includes the required section in the correct order, even if some fields render fallback content.
reproduction.actual_behavior: The section is skipped and the output no longer matches the required structure.

logs: Rendered output contains later sections but the summary block is missing

manual_checks: Generate the report fixture again and confirm the required summary section is present
Visually compare the section order before and after the fix

rollback.rollback_rule: Rollback on any hard-check failure or if the export format changes for unrelated sections
rollback.notes: Preserve the last stable report rendering output for comparison.

scope_notes: Stay within report assembly and the affected template logic. Do not rewrite the entire export pipeline or unrelated templates.

notes.root_cause_hint: A conditional render may be tied to an optional field instead of the section requirement itself
notes.risk_hint: A naive fallback could duplicate the section or alter report ordering
similar_bugs: footer hidden when disclaimer text is empty
chart block skipped when one datapoint is null
